dE4dK8LehML1Py8FowF"]},"/home/lojak/Desktop/x3-chain-master/contracts/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/introspection/IERC165.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8891738ffe910f0cf2da09566928589bf5d63f4524dd734fd9cedbac3274dd5c","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://971f954442df5c2ef5b5ebf1eb245d7105d9fbacc7386ee5c796df1d45b21617","dweb:/ipfs/QmadRjHbkicwqwwh61raUEapaVEtaLMcYbQZWs9gUkgj3u"]},"src/libraries/MathLibraries.sol":{"keccak256":"0x0d152a56ccfde2269d3c2f8dbacfc194830537069768ab4fc37ccfc5d20b3209","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://33253274311815109e7c9d61251052940ec1358aff8b10ec27a77f143d2696ef","dweb:/ipfs/QmTYRK2Nwi3uyfep1qFdsqLBHTwTq1oHsY8JPuCMFQoTQy"]},"src/tokens/AToken.sol":{"keccak256":"0x0b2e999feca232ab85156237b40d2f5cac33d936474939d692ada4e38af36010","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://e32f0a0070e5434e474cbe8e82aedb6e8b68a966cf3fec0c3cff8a45f85404ce","dweb:/ipfs/QmNv8y7jc9UxcNneY1TffxAi7ZoZBi5zN6g1YFL6oqHU9U"]}},"version":1}

allowance(address,address).details: Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

decimals().details: Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the default value returned by this function, unless it's overridden. NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}.

returns._0: True if this is the user's first deposit

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

transfer(address,uint256).details: Overrides ERC20 to handle scaled balances

balanceOf(address).notice: Returns the actual balance including accrued interest

burn(address,address,uint256,uint256).notice: Burn aTokens and send underlying to recipient

rescueTokens(address,address,uint256).notice: Rescue tokens accidentally sent to this contract

scaledBalanceOf(address).notice: Returns the scaled balance (constant, for accounting)

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).notice: Transfer aTokens from one address to another

transferUnderlyingTo(address,uint256).notice: Transfer underlying held by this contract (used by pool for flashloans)

documentation.text: @notice Underlying asset this aToken represents
@notice Pool contract (only minter/burner)
@notice Current liquidity index (RAY precision)
@notice Treasury address for protocol fees
@notice Incentives controller for rewards
@notice Scaled balances (constant, doesn't grow with interest)
@notice Total scaled supply

@notice Transfer aTokens to another user

@notice Transfer aTokens from one address to another
@notice Internal transfer with scaled balance handling
@notice Total supply including accrued interest
@notice Total scaled supply

@notice Get current liquidity index

@notice Handle underlying received (for repayments)
 @param amount Amount received

@title AToken
 @notice Interest-bearing deposit receipt token
 @dev User deposits underlying → receives aTokens representing their share
 Key mechanics:
 - Scaled balance: Internal balance that stays constant
 - Balance: Returns scaled * liquidityIndex (increases over time)
 - Interest accrues passively through index growth
 Security:
 - Only pool can mint/burn
 - Transfer validations for collateral usage
 - Reentrancy protection via CEI pattern
